data.title: Gold posts biggest one-day dollar gain on record; Deutsche Bank says gold could hit $6,000
data.description:
Gold futures rallied to yet another all-time high Wednesday, soaring by more than $220/oz for the metal's largest one-day dollar gain in history, as a weakening dollar continued to push investors toward hard assets.

The U.S. dollar index hit a four-year low on Tuesday and suffered its steepest one-day drop since last year's tariff rollout after President Trump said he thought the dollar was "doing great" and had not weakened excessively.

The dollar's recent decline is great for U.S. businesses, Trump told reporters in Iowa, and while the president's remarks were in line with previous commentary from U.S. officials, currency markets were rattled because the comments seemed to validate the greenback's steep decline in recent sessions.

"While a softer USD benefits exporters - it costs foreign buyers less to purchase dollars - it can increase inflationary pressures," FP Markets chief analyst Aaron Hill said in a note. "The issue right now is Trump's unpredictability. From threatening tariffs to designs on Greenland, his actions continue to rattle the markets, prompting investors to reduce exposure to U.S. assets. This adds fuel to USD downside and could ultimately bolster inflation."

Markets also remain sensitive to recent turmoil in Japanese bond markets, which has raised concerns about liquidity risks across the global financial system.

Some analysts also noted that Trump could select BlackRock's head of global fixed income Rick Rieder, who favors aggressive rate cuts, as the next Fed Chair.

Gold prices could climb to $6,000/oz this year in a weak U.S. dollar environment, Deutsche Bank analysts said in a note, as gold's continued rise "reflects investment motives which may be persistent: higher reserve allocations and investors raising allocations to non-dollar and real assets."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +4.3% to $5,301.60/oz, its seventh consecutive daily gain - up 15.5% in that time - and largest one-day percentage gain since March 24, 2020, while front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +7.2% to $113.111/oz, its second-highest settlement value in history.

Gold added to gains after-hours, topping $5,400 even as the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as expected.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: IC Group grants 0.29M stock options
data.description:
• IC Group Holdings (ICGH:CA) on Wednesday had granted 0.29M stock options exercisable at $0.65 per share with immediate vesting.
  • The grant included 0.12M options with a ten-year term to January 28, 2036, and 0.17M options with a 30-month term to July 28, 2028.
  • As part of a long-term incentive, the company granted 0.12M options to a new employee, exercisable at $0.81 and expiring January 28, 2036.
  • Shares +6.67%.
  • Press release.

data.title: Waste Management stock slips after Q4 earnings miss
data.description:
Shares of Waste Management (WM) fell 2% in extended trading Wednesday after the trash and recycling company reported fourth-quarter earnings that fell short of Wall Street expectations.

The company’s adjusted earnings were $1.93 a share, below the consensus estimate of $1.95 a share. Revenue for the three-month period was $6.31 billion, short of the $6.39 billion expected by analysts.

 

 

Net income rose to $742 million, or $1.83 a share, from $598 million, or $1.48 a share, a year earlier.

Waste Management (WM) Chief Executive Jim Fish said in a statement that 2025 reflected “disciplined execution” as the company improved operating expenses as a percentage of revenue.

Operational gains lift profitability

Waste Management (WM) said income from operations increased to $1.16 billion from $919 million a year earlier. Operating margins also improved, supported by cost controls and efficiency initiatives.

The company reported operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of $1.93 billion, compared with $1.57 billion a year earlier. On an adjusted basis, ebitda was $1.97 billion and the margin improved to 31.3% from 28.9%.

Cash flow rises, growth investments continue

Waste Management (WM) said net cash provided by operating activities increased 12.1% in 2025 to $6.04 billion. Free cash flow rose 26.8% to $2.94 billion.

The company pointed to progress in recycling automation, renewable natural gas and its healthcare solutions business. Waste Management (WM) said it completed seven renewable natural gas facilities and nine recycling automation and growth projects during 2025.

2026 outlook

Waste Management (WM) forecast 2026 revenue of $26.43 billion to $26.63 billion and adjusted operating ebitda of $8.15 billion to $8.25 billion. The company also projected free cash flow of $3.75 billion to $3.85 billion.

Fish said Waste Management (WM) expects to return about $3.5 billion to shareholders in 2026 through dividends and share repurchases.

data.title: AstraZeneca signs research agreement with University of Virginia
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (AZN) has signed a master research collaboration agreement with the University of Virginia designed to accelerate pre-clinical research.
  • The partnership will "enhance the pace of discovery and translational research in areas spanning oncology, immunology, cardiovascular, metabolism and infectious disease research," according to UVA.
  • The first program under the agreement will be to conduct preclinical research for C. difficile.
  • UVA will also leverage its capabilities in high-throughput screening, advanced imaging, computational modeling, and biostatistics.
  • In October 2025, AstraZeneca said it would invest several billion dollars on a new manufacturing plant near Charlottesville, Va., where UVA is located.

data.title: CopperCorp enters marketing services agreement
data.description:
• CopperCorp Resources (CPER:CA) said on Wednesday it entered a consulting services agreement with Capital Analytica on Jan. 27, 2026, granting options for 0.2M common shares at $0.10 each, exercisable over three years.
  • The company paid $0.075M on signing a six-month deal with Capital Analytica and $0.075M by April 27, 2026, and has an option to extend for six months at $0.075M.
  • Jeff French, arms-length to the company, is the principal of Capital Analytica and will oversee all services under the agreement.

data.title: Liberty Energy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.15 beats by $0.22, revenue of $4B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Liberty Energy press release (LBRT): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $0.15 beats by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $4B (-7.2% Y/Y) beats by $150M.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenue of $1.0 billion and net income of $14 million, or $0.08 fully diluted earnings per share
  • Fourth quarter 2025 Adjusted EBITDA1 of $158 million
  • Raised quarterly cash dividend by 13% to $0.09 per share beginning in the fourth quarter of 2025
  • Shares -1.83% AH.

data.title: Oil States signs new $125M credit agreement
data.description:
• Oil States International (NYSE:OIS) announced on Wednesday that it entered into a new credit agreement providing total commitments of $125M, including a $75M revolving facility and a $50M multi-draw term loan available through July 28, 2026.
  • The maturity date of the Cash Flow Credit Agreement is January 28, 2030.
  • The company said it bought back $50M of its 4.75% convertible senior notes due 2026 in Q4 2025, and $53M remained outstanding at year-end.
  • The company plans to retire the remaining notes using cash and credit facility borrowings, with cash totaling $70M and no borrowings outstanding.
  • Shares -2.33%
